en: For we know that if the tent, which is our earthly home, is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
zh: 我們知道，如果我們在地上的帳棚拆毀了，我們必得著從 神而來的居所。那不是人手所造的，而是天上永存的房屋。

en: For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling,
zh: 我們現今在這帳棚裡面歎息，渴望遷到那天上的住處，好像換上新的衣服；

en: if indeed by putting it on we may not be found naked.
zh: 如果穿上了，就不會赤身出現了。

en: For while we are still in this tent, we groan, being burdened--not that we would be unclothed, but that we would be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
zh: 我們這些在帳棚裡面的人，勞苦歎息，是由於不願意脫去這個，卻願意穿上那個，使這必死的被生命吞滅。

en: He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
zh: 那在我們身上完成了工作，使我們達成這目標的，就是 神。他已經把聖靈賜給我們作憑據。

en: So we are always of good courage. We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord,
zh: 我們既然一向都是坦然無懼的，又知道住在身內就是與主分開 ，

en: for we walk by faith, not by sight.
Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
zh: 現在還是坦然無懼，寧願與身體分開，與主同住。

en: So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please him.
zh: 因此，我們立定志向，無論住在身內或是與身體分開，都要討主的喜悅。

en: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may receive what is due for what he has done in the body, whether good or evil.
zh: 因為我們眾人都必須在基督的審判臺前顯露出來，使各人按著本身所行的，或善或惡，受到報應。

en: Therefore, knowing the fear of the Lord, we persuade others. But what we are is known to God, and I hope it is known also to your conscience.
zh: 我們既然知道主是可畏的，就勸勉眾人。我們在 神面前是顯明的，我盼望在你們的良心裡也是顯明的。

en: We are not commending ourselves to you again but giving you cause to boast about us, so that you may be able to answer those who boast about outward appearance and not about what is in the heart.
zh: 我們不是又再向你們推薦自己，而是給你們機會以我們為樂，使你們可以應付那些只誇外貌不誇內心的人。

en: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God; if we are in our right mind, it is for you.
zh: 如果我們瘋狂，那是為了 神；如果我們清醒，那是為了你們。

en: For the love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: that one has died for all, therefore all have died;
zh: 原來基督的愛催逼著我們，因為我們斷定一個人替眾人死了，眾人就都死了。

en: and he died for all, that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for him who for their sake died and was raised.
zh: 他替眾人死了，為的是要使活著的人不再為自己活著，卻為那替他們死而復活的主而活。

en: From now on, therefore, we regard no one according to the flesh. Even though we once regarded Christ according to the flesh, we regard him thus no longer.
zh: 所以，從今以後，我們不再按照人的看法認識人；雖然我們曾經按照人的看法認識基督，但現在不再這樣了。

en: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.
zh: 如果有人在基督裡，他就是新造的人，舊事已經過去，你看，都變成新的了！

en: All this is from God, who through Christ reconciled us to himself and gave us the ministry of reconciliation;
zh: 這一切都是出於 神，他藉著基督使我們與他自己和好，並且把這和好的職分賜給我們，

en: that is, in Christ God was reconciling the world to himself, not counting their trespasses against them, and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation.
zh: 就是 神在基督裡使世人與他自己和好，不再追究他們的過犯，並且把和好的道理託付了我們。

en: Therefore, we are ambassadors for Christ, God making his appeal through us. We implore you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
zh: 因此，我們就是基督的使者， 神藉著我們勸告世人。我們代替基督請求你們：跟 神和好吧！

en: For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.
zh: 神使那無罪的替我們成為有罪的，使我們在他裡面成為 神的義。
